headline: NHAI Extends ‘Clean Toilet Picture Challenge’ till 30th June 2027
fullText:
Ministry of Road Transport & Highways

NHAI Extends ‘Clean Toilet Picture Challenge’ till 30th June 2027

Initiative received enthusiastic response from the National Highway users with 429 commuters rewarded with FASTag recharge for reporting unclean toilets in 2026 till date

23 JUL 2026 7:30PM by PIB Delhi

With an objective to further enhance cleanliness and user experience at toilet facilities along the National Highways, NHAI has extended its citizen-centric initiative, the ‘Clean Toilet Picture Challenge’ till 30th June 2027. The extension of the initiative aims to ensure continued public participation and effective implementation of the ‘Clean Toilet Picture’ scheme, along with timely processing of FASTag recharge reward linked to the initiative. The initiative received enthusiastic response from the National Highway users with around 429 commuters reporting unclean toilets have been rewarded with FASTag recharge worth Rs. 1,000 each in 2026 so far.

Under the campaign, unclean toilet pictures shared by National Highway users are assessed and eligible claims under the scheme are processed in a time-bound manner. FASTag recharge reward is credited to the linked Vehicle Registration Number (VRN), thereby enhancing transparency and responsiveness.

NHAI launched the unique ‘Clean Toilet Picture Challenge’ in September 2025 to encourage National Highway users to actively report unclean toilet facilities at the toll plazas along National Highways. The initiative is open for all National Highway commuters, who can participate by uploading clear, geo-tagged, and time-stamped photographs of unclean toilets through the latest version of the Rajmargyatra Mobile App. Users are also required to provide basic details including name, location, vehicle registration number and mobile number while submitting the picture.

Each eligible vehicle registration number reporting such an instance will receive a reward of Rs. 1,000 (Rupees One Thousand only) in the form of a FASTag recharge credited to the linked VRN. Each VRN is eligible for only one reward during the entire scheme period. The reward is non-transferable and cannot be claimed in cash. In cases where multiple reports are submitted for the same facility on the same day, only the first valid submission through the Rajmargyatra App is considered.

The scheme is applicable exclusively to toilet facilities constructed, operated, or maintained under NHAI’s jurisdiction. Toilets located at retail fuel stations, dhabas, or other public facilities that are not under NHAI’s control have been excluded. All entries are subject to verification through AI-assisted screening and manual validation, wherever required.

With this initiative, NHAI reiterates its commitment to enhance citizen-centric services and foster a culture of shared responsibility of maintaining cleanliness and hygiene along the National Highways.

headline: OPERATION SOUTHERN READINESS 26-2 CONCLUDES AT SOUTHERN NAVAL COMMAND, KOCHI
fullText:
Ministry of Defence

OPERATION SOUTHERN READINESS 26-2 CONCLUDES AT SOUTHERN NAVAL COMMAND, KOCHI

23 JUL 2026 7:25PM by PIB Delhi

Operation Southern Readiness 26-2, a four-day multinational maritime security training engagement, concluded at Southern Naval Command, Kochi on 23 Jul 2026. Conducted by the Indian Navy in partnership with Combined Maritime Forces (CMF) under the aegis of Southern Naval Command and the Indian Navy-led Combined Task Force 154, the event brought together 276 personnel from 27 countries, supported by 76 facilitators and Subject Matter Experts, with participation from four international organisations.

The engagement was inaugurated on 20 Jul 2026 by RAdm Deepak Singhal, Chief Staff Officer (Training), Headquarters Southern Naval Command, in the presence of Cmde Dan Thomas, Deputy Commander, Combined Maritime Forces, senior officers, and representatives of participating nations. The Closing Ceremony, held on 23 Jul 2026, featured a closing address by Cmde Milind M Mokashi, Commander CTF 154, presentation of certificates to the facilitators, and a valedictory address by the Chief Staff Officer (Training).

The programme brought together maritime security, legal and operational perspectives through professional discussions, simulator-based instructions, and practical training. Sessions addressed Maritime Domain Awareness and Information Sharing, Maritime Law, Counter-Narcotics, Force Protection Measures, Asymmetric Threats, Maritime Uncrewed Systems, and Operational Sea Training. The domain-specific training was facilitated through inputs from the Indian Navy, including the Information Fusion Centre - Indian Ocean Region (IFC-IOR), CMF elements such as the Joint Maritime Information Center (JMIC), and specialist international organisations including the United Nations Office on Drugs and Crime (UNODC) and the IMO International Maritime Law Institute (IMO IMLI).

Participants also undertook practical training in Damage Control and Firefighting, maritime communications, Survival at Sea, and Boarding procedures, besides sea training onboard Indian Naval ship. These activities provided opportunities for participants to exchange experiences and Best Practices, contributing to building greater understanding across maritime services and organisations.

The engagement strengthened interoperability, professional networks, and operational confidence among participating nations. It also contributed to regional capacity-building and collective maritime security by combining theoretical understanding with hands-on training. The four-day engagement generated 3,800 training man-hours.

Operation Southern Readiness 26-2 demonstrated the Indian Navy’s modern training infrastructure, state-of-the-art simulators, advanced instructional methodologies, and professional expertise. The successful conduct of the exercise has reinforced the Indian Navy-CMF partnership and has underscored Indian Navy’s leadership of CTF 154 whilst highlighting Southern Naval Command’s role as a leading training hub and preferred destination for professional maritime training.

_____________________________________________________________

(Release ID: 2288511)

headline: Bharat Renewable Energy Summit & Expo (BRESE) 2026 to be Held from 2–5 November in New Delhi: Shri Pralhad Joshi
fullText:
Ministry of New and Renewable Energy

Bharat Renewable Energy Summit & Expo (BRESE) 2026 to be Held from 2–5 November in New Delhi: Shri Pralhad Joshi

Summit to be held alongside the 9th Session of the International Solar Alliance (ISA) Assembly and the 4th International Conference on Green Hydrogen

23 JUL 2026 7:23PM by PIB Delhi

The Bharat Renewable Energy Summit & Expo (BRESE) 2026 will be held from 2nd to 5th November 2026 at Bharat Mandapam, New Delhi, said Shri Pralhad Joshi, Union Minister for New and Renewable Energy and Consumer Affairs, Food and Public Distribution, while addressing the Curtain Raiser of the BRESE 2026 here today.

In his keynote address, Shri Pralhad Joshi said that the BRESE 2026 will, for the first time, bring together three major renewable energy events under one platform—BRESE 2026, the 9th Session of the International Solar Alliance (ISA) Assembly, and the 4th International Conference on Green Hydrogen (ICGH). Shri Joshi further informed that the Prime Minister has agreed to inaugurate BRESE 2026. He said the Prime Minister's presence reflects India's commitment to renewable energy as a key pillar of Viksit Bharat and its contribution to global sustainable development.

Shri Joshi further stated that the Summit will bring together leading renewable energy developers, technology companies, manufacturers, battery storage firms, green hydrogen pioneers, financial institutions, international organisations, and investors, ensuring representation of the entire renewable energy value chain under one roof. He added that the Summit has also been designed with a truly global outlook. Invitations have been extended to 34 countries for the BRESE Summit, while the International Solar Alliance has invited 128 countries for the Assembly.

Shri Joshi emphasized that India’s credibility on the global stage is built on its strong track record of delivery, which has enabled it to convene the global renewable energy community. He further stated that India will soon cross the landmark of 300 GW of installed non-fossil fuel capacity. He also informed that the PM Surya Ghar: Muft Bijli Yojana is expected to cross 50 lakh beneficiary households in the coming weeks.

Concluding his address, the Union Minister described the Bharat Renewable Energy Summit & Expo 2026 as a significant milestone in India’s engagement with the global renewable energy community. He said the Summit reflects the growth of the sector, emerging opportunities, and India’s commitment to global collaboration in advancing the clean energy transition. He extended a warm invitation to governments, industry leaders, investors, researchers, innovators, entrepreneurs, and development partners worldwide to participate in the Summit at Bharat Mandapam, New Delhi, from 2nd to 5th November 2026. Shri Joshi expressed confidence that the Summit will foster new partnerships, catalyse investments, promote innovation, and strengthen international cooperation in the renewable energy sector.

During the Curtain Raiser, Shri Joshi also unveiled the official logo of BRESE 2026, ‘URJHA Chakra’, and launched the official digital portal and mobile application for the Summit.

Shri Shripad Yesso Naik, Minister of State for Power and New and Renewable Energy, highlighted that India's renewable energy transition is being driven by the collective efforts of States across the country. He noted that while Rajasthan is harnessing its vast solar resources and Gujarat is developing the world's largest hybrid renewable energy park at Khavda, several other States are advancing hydropower, floating solar, biomass and decentralised renewable energy, reflecting the spirit of cooperative and competitive federalism.

Shri Santosh Kumar Sarangi, Secretary, Ministry of New and Renewable Energy, said that India's renewable energy transformation has been built on four key pillars—policy stability, market creation, ecosystem development and transmission infrastructure. He said the next phase of the energy transition will focus on strengthening system capabilities through hybrid renewable energy projects, battery energy storage systems, floating solar, agrivoltaics and the National Green Hydrogen Mission. He added that Bharat Renewable Energy Summit & Expo (BRESE) 2026 will provide a common platform for governments, industry, investors, innovators and international partners to accelerate investments, technology partnerships and collaboration in clean energy.

Several national and international delegates, along with senior officers of the Ministry, were present during the event.

headline: GUIDELINES FOR URBAN CHALLENGE FUND
fullText:
Ministry of Housing & Urban Affairs

GUIDELINES FOR URBAN CHALLENGE FUND

23 JUL 2026 7:18PM by PIB Delhi

The Ministry of Housing and Urban Affairs has launched Operational Guidelines for the Urban Challenge Fund (UCF) on 15th April, 2026. The scheme guidelines are in public domain and may be accessed at https://www.mohua.gov.in/static/uploads/2026/04/7950afb9b2d5cb773ba3d6c64bde9726.pdf

UCF has been introduced to catalyse a paradigm shift in urban development by encouraging cities to transition towards market-based financing and integrated regional development. The Fund aims to position cities as a bankable asset class through targeted reforms, strengthened institutional capacity, improved governance and planning frameworks, and the development of sustainable revenue-generating urban infrastructure.

Under UCF, private sector participation is governed by the respective State/ Union Territories (UT) Governments and the Urban Local Bodies (ULBs). As per UCF Guidelines, projects under UCF may be implemented by private entities endorsed by the State/UT Government. Further, ULBs, in their administrative jurisdiction, can designate the private entities as “Special Purpose Urban Infrastructure Manager (SPUIM)” for integrated delivery of housing, infrastructure, and Operations and Maintenance (O&M) in large-scale development.

Following the launch of the Operational Guidelines of UCF, the Union Government has been actively engaged with States/ UTs. In addition to this, the Hon'ble Minister for Housing and Urban Affairs held a strategic meeting with the Ministers-in-charge of the Urban Development Departments of States/UTs on April 25, 2026, in New Delhi. The discussions focused on assisting States and UTs to effectively utilize the ₹1 lakh crore central assistance earmarked under the fund.

This information was given by the Minister of State for Housing & Urban Affairs, Shri Tokhan Sahu in a written reply in the Lok Sabha today.

(Release ID: 2288504)

headline: IMPLEMENTATION OF PM-SVANIDHI SCHEME
fullText:
Ministry of Housing & Urban Affairs

IMPLEMENTATION OF PM-SVANIDHI SCHEME

23 JUL 2026 7:17PM by PIB Delhi

The PM SVANidhi Scheme was initially implemented in statutory urban areas. The eligibility was also extended to street vendors from surrounding peri-urban areas who vend within the geographical limits of Urban Local Bodies (ULBs). Under the restructured PM SVANidhi Scheme, the coverage of the Scheme is expanded in a phased manner beyond statutory urban areas to include census towns, peri urban areas etc.

Impact assessment studies of the PM SVANidhi scheme was undertaken by Indian School of Business (ISB), Hyderabad in 2023 & 2025. The 2023 study was conducted in 100 Urban Local Bodies (ULBs) across the country covering more than 5,000 beneficiaries and it found that PM SVANidhi was the first loan from a bank for 95% of the beneficiaries and the loan was utilized for expanding their business. The 2025 study was conducted in 99 Urban Local Bodies (ULBs) across the country covering more than 5,000 beneficiaries (with around 60% of the respondents being part of the 2023 study sample) and it found that the average annualized business income among SVANidhi borrowers grew by around 20 percent, between 2023 and 2025.

Around 30 percent of borrowers across all cycles reported holding loans other than PM SVANidhi loans which underscores the scheme’s role in building credit histories for vendors who previously had little or no access to formal credit. Further the study also found that between 2023 and 2025, the adoption of digital payments among street vendors rose significantly.

The study also found the scheme’s impact extends well beyond business recovery. By easing credit constraints and stabilizing household cash flows, the scheme has enabled vendor families to improve living conditions, strengthen food security, enhance access to health-care, and support children’s education.

The identification of street vendors is carried out by States/UTs through surveys conducted under the provisions of the Street Vendors (Protection of Livelihood and Regulation of Street Vending) Act, 2014. The responsibility for conducting surveys, issuing Certificates of Vending etc. rests with the respective Urban Local Bodies and State/UT Governments.

Number of Letters of Recommendation (LoR) issued, Tranche-wise loans sanctioned and disbursed in Katni, Panna and Khajuraho during the last three years (FY 2023-24 to FY 2025-26) is provided in Annexure – I.

UPI-linked RuPay Credit Card has been introduced for beneficiaries who have repaid the second loan tranche. The UPI-linked RuPay Credit Card was launched on 23rd January 2026, and as on 12th July, 2026, number of credit cards issued in Katni, Panna and Khajuraho cities are 14, 2 and 1 respectively.

All street vendors including, seasonal, mobile, women and tourism-dependent vendors are covered under the PM SVANidhi scheme provided they satisfy the eligibility criteria prescribed under the scheme guidelines.

State/ULBs are responsible for identification of eligible street vendors and mobilisation of new applications under the scheme. As per PM SVANidhi Portal, the average time taken to sanction loan is 23 days. To enhance coverage and ensure wider inclusion of street vendor beneficiaries, the Ministry has undertaken several initiatives, including periodic awareness campaigns through radio jingles, television and newspaper advertisements. States and Union Territories are also regularly provided with Information, Education and Communication (IEC) materials in local languages to facilitate outreach and dissemination of scheme benefits among street vendors. Regular reviews are also conducted with States/UTs/ULBs and banks /lending institutions.

Under the scheme, MoHUA has organized various campaigns and events to enhance outreach to street vendors. After the approval of the restructured PM SVANidhi scheme on 27th August 2025, Lok Kalyan Melas were organized in ULBs from 17th September 2025 to 15th October 2025. Additionally, a special campaign was conducted from 3rd November, 2025 to 2nd December, 2025 across all States and Union Territories in a mission mode to clear pendency at both the bank and ULB levels. ULBs, in collaboration with Digital Payment Aggregators (DPAs), also conduct regular digital literacy camps. To enhance digital onboarding and promote the adoption of cashless payments among street vendors, beneficiaries are provided cashback incentives. A nationwide Special Campaign was also launched by the Ministry from 1st June to 30th June 2026, comprising SVANidhi Mahotsav and Lok Kalyan Melas across States and Union Territories. These Melas promoted the new features of the schemes and supported vendor mobilisation, loan application submission, faster loan disbursement, and digital onboarding of beneficiaries.

Further, the Ministry has issued guidelines to States/UTs for the development of Street Food Hubs under PM SVANidhi to create well-planned, safe and vibrant vending spaces, particularly at locations with high public and tourist footfall.

This information was given by the Minister of State for Housing & Urban Affairs, Shri Tokhan Sahu in a written reply in the Lok Sabha today.

headline: NATIONAL FRAMEWORK FOR STRUCTURAL SAFETY AUDITS AND SEISMIC RESILIENCE OF BUILDINGS
fullText:
Ministry of Housing & Urban Affairs

NATIONAL FRAMEWORK FOR STRUCTURAL SAFETY AUDITS AND SEISMIC RESILIENCE OF BUILDINGS

23 JUL 2026 7:16PM by PIB Delhi

As per 12th Schedule of the Constitution of India, urban planning including building plan approvals and enforcement of building bye-laws, is the function of Urban Local Bodies (ULBs)/ Urban Development Authorities. Government of India supplements the efforts of the States through schematic interventions/ advisories. The Government of India supplements the efforts of States and Union Territories through model regulations, technical standards, guidelines and advisories. The Ministry of Housing & Urban Affairs (MoHUA) has published Model Building Bye-laws (MBBL) – 2016 (https://www.mohua.gov.in/static/uploads/2026/07/0e33558a9326c8a89f945cae00cc3647.pdf), in which Chapter-6 deals with Provisions for Structural Safety, and lays down guidelines/standards relating to structural safety and seismic resilience of buildings.

Further, the Bureau of Indian Standards (BIS), under the Ministry of Consumer Affairs, Food and Public Distribution, Government of India, has formulated various standards relating to earthquake resistant design and construction of buildings and structures, and for seismic evaluation, repair and strengthening of buildings such as IS 1893 (Criteria for Earthquake Resistant Design of Structures), IS 4326:2013 (Earthquake resistant design and construction of buildings – Code of Practice (third revision) and other allied codes for adoption and enforcement by States/UTs and Urban Local Bodies as part of their building regulations.

Further, the National Disaster Management Authority (NDMA), under the Ministry of Home Affairs (MHA), has issued the National Disaster Management Guidelines on Management of Earthquakes, which provide guidance on earthquake risk reduction, including measures for safer construction, structural strengthening and retrofitting of buildings, particularly lifeline and critical infrastructure (https://ndma.gov.in/sites/default/files/PDF/Guidelines/earthquakes.pdf).

Regulation and enforcement relating to periodic structural and seismic safety audits, building inspection regimes and compliance requirements are within the domain of States/Union Territories (UTs) and ULBs.

This information was given by the Minister of State for Housing & Urban Affairs, Shri Tokhan Sahu in a written reply in the Lok Sabha today.

(Release ID: 2288502)

headline: ROLE OF INTEGRATED COMMAND AND CONTROL CENTRES IN SMART CITIES
fullText:
Ministry of Housing & Urban Affairs

ROLE OF INTEGRATED COMMAND AND CONTROL CENTRES IN SMART CITIES

23 JUL 2026 7:13PM by PIB Delhi

The Integrated Command and Control Centres (ICCCs) have played a crucial role in the implementation of the Smart Cities Mission. These centres play a vital role in urban management by enabling real-time monitoring, data-driven decision-making, and integrated service delivery across various sectors such as mobility, water supply, sanitation, solid waste management, public safety, etc.

Under the Smart Cities Mission, Integrated Command and Control Centres (ICCCs) have been operationalised across all 100 Smart Cities. These centres play a vital role in urban management by enabling real-time monitoring, data-driven decision-making, and integrated service delivery across key sectors such as mobility, water supply, sanitation, solid waste management and public safety.

ICCCs are functioning as the nerve centers for city operations, enabling real-time monitoring, data-driven decision-making and proactive management of urban services. These centres integrate data from various urban domains such as traffic management, solid waste management, water supply, street lighting, public safety and emergency response systems. Key areas where ICCCs are being effectively utilised includes Traffic and Mobility Management, Solid Waste Management, Emergency and Disaster Response, Citizen Grievance Redressal, Public Safety and Surveillance, etc.

The data-driven insights generated by ICCCs are being increasingly leveraged to inform urban planning and policy decisions, thereby enhancing urban resilience and improving the quality of life for citizens. Specific examples include the use of Intelligent Traffic Management System (ITMS), Automatic Number Plate Recognition (ANPR) for traffic management and enforcement, GPS-based route optimisation for efficient solid waste collection, facial recognition systems for enhanced public safety and crowd monitoring technologies to manage large gatherings and ensure citizen security. These tools enable evidence-based governance, improve operational efficiency and support proactive decision making at the city level.

This information was given by the Minister of State for Housing & Urban Affairs, Shri Tokhan Sahu in a written reply in the Lok Sabha today.

headline: NICDC Organises Cross-Learning Site Visit to AURIC Smart City to Strengthen Industrial Corridor Development
fullText:
Ministry of Commerce & Industry

NICDC Organises Cross-Learning Site Visit to AURIC Smart City to Strengthen Industrial Corridor Development

23 JUL 2026 7:09PM by PIB Delhi

National Industrial Corridor Development Corporation (NICDC), in collaboration with Maharashtra Industrial Township Limited (MITL), commenced a two-day cross-learning site visit to the Shendra-Bidkin Industrial Area (AURIC Smart City), Chhatrapati Sambhajinagar, Maharashtra, on 23 July 2026.

The programme has brought together senior officials from State Governments, Special Purpose Vehicles (SPVs), Programme Managers for New Cities (PMNCs), EPC contractors and other stakeholders associated with Industrial Corridor projects across the country to facilitate knowledge exchange, strengthen institutional capacities and showcase best practices in the planning, implementation and operation of Greenfield Industrial Smart Cities.

Addressing the participants, Chief Executive Officer & Managing Director, NICDC, Shri Rajat Kumar Saini, highlighted that AURIC has emerged as a model for integrated industrial city development under the National Industrial Corridor Development Programme (NICDP). He emphasised that the project demonstrates the importance of world-class infrastructure, investor-centric planning, technology-enabled governance and multimodal connectivity in creating globally competitive manufacturing ecosystems. He also underlined the importance of empowering Special Purpose Vehicles with greater planning flexibility to enable efficient land utilisation, infrastructure optimisation and future expansion. Shri Saini noted that cross-learning initiatives play an important role in replicating successful implementation practices across industrial corridor projects.

The first day of the programme provided participants with first-hand exposure to AURIC’s planning approach and integrated infrastructure. The visit included presentations on the development of the Shendra-Bidkin Industrial Area, demonstrations of digital planning and monitoring systems, and visits to operational industrial units and key infrastructure facilities, including utility systems, connectivity infrastructure and industrial development sites.

The programme also provided a platform for discussions on integrated master planning, project implementation, land utilisation, investor facilitation, digital governance and project monitoring. Participants exchanged experiences on strengthening coordination among implementing agencies and adopting best practices to accelerate infrastructure development across Industrial Corridor projects.

The visit also included discussions on the implementation framework of the Bharat Audyogik Vikas Yojana (BHAVYA), a flagship initiative being implemented by NICDC to develop world-class plug-and-play industrial parks across the country. Participants deliberated on how the experience gained from AURIC's integrated planning, utility infrastructure, digital governance and investor facilitation mechanisms could support implementation of industrial parks under the BHAVYA Scheme. States and implementing agencies were encouraged to actively participate in the first round of applications under the scheme, with submissions open until 31 July 2026.

The discussions highlighted the role of plug-and-play infrastructure, integrated utility networks, technology-enabled urban management and multimodal connectivity in attracting investments, improving ease of doing business and strengthening India's manufacturing ecosystem. Participants also exchanged views on sustainable infrastructure, digital land management systems, investor-friendly approval mechanisms and future-ready industrial ecosystems that could be adapted across other Industrial Corridor projects.

The programme will continue on 24 July 2026 with technical presentations on project implementation, infrastructure development, challenges and best practices, followed by an interactive cross-learning session among participating States and project stakeholders. The deliberations are expected to further strengthen institutional collaboration and facilitate adoption of best practices across Industrial Corridor projects being developed under the National Industrial Corridor Development Programme (NICDP).

headline: LOK SABHA SPEAKER PAYS FLORAL TRIBUTES TO LOKMANYA BAL GANGADHAR TILAK
fullText:
Lok Sabha Secretariat

LOK SABHA SPEAKER PAYS FLORAL TRIBUTES TO LOKMANYA BAL GANGADHAR TILAK

23 JUL 2026 6:55PM by PIB Delhi

New Delhi; 23 July 2026: Lok Sabha Speaker Shri Om Birla paid floral tributes at the portrait of Lokmanya Bal Gangadhar Tilak in the Central Hall of Samvidhan Sadan on the occasion of his birth anniversary, today.

Members of Parliament; former Members and other dignitaries also offered floral tributes to Lokmanya Bal Gangadhar Tilak on this occasion. Secretary-General, Lok Sabha, Shri Utpal Kumar Singh also paid tributes to Lokmanya Bal Gangadhar Tilak.

Widely known as ‘Lokmanya’, Shri Bal Gangadhar Tilak was a towering nationalist, visionary leader, social reformer, and scholar. He played a pioneering role in mobilizing political consciousness among the masses during the Indian freedom struggle and was a staunch advocate of self-rule (Swaraj). His stirring call— “Swaraj is my birthright, and I shall have it!”—galvanized generations of Indians to rise against colonial rule.

The portrait of Lokmanya Tilak was unveiled in the Central Hall of Samvidhan Sadan (then Parliament House) on 28 July 1956 by the then Prime Minister Pandit Jawaharlal Nehru, in recognition of his extraordinary contributions to the nation.

headline: STATUS OF JUDICIAL APPOINTMENTS AND JUDICIAL REFORMS
fullText:
Ministry of Law and Justice

STATUS OF JUDICIAL APPOINTMENTS AND JUDICIAL REFORMS

23 JUL 2026 6:45PM by PIB Delhi

Appointments of judges in the Supreme Court and High Courts are governed by Articles 124, 217 and 224 of the Constitution of India.

The procedure for appointment of Judges of the Supreme Court and High Courts is laid down in the Memorandum of Procedure (MoP) prepared in 1998 pursuant to the Supreme Court Judgment of October 6, 1993 (Second Judges case) read with their Advisory Opinion of October 28, 1998 (Third Judges case). As per the MoP, initiation of proposal for appointment of Judges in the Supreme Court vests with the Chief Justice of India and initiation of proposal for appointment of Judges in the High Courts vests with the Chief Justice of the concerned High Court. Only those persons are appointed as Judges of High Courts and the Supreme Court who are recommended by the Supreme Court Collegium (SCC).

Appointment of the Judges of the Constitutional Courts is a continuous, integrated and collaborative process between the Executive and the Judiciary. It requires consultation and approval from various constitutional authorities both at state and central level.

In order to replace the Collegium system of appointments of Judges of the Supreme Court and High Courts with a more broad-based, transparent, accountable appointment mechanism and to bring greater objectivity in the system, the Constitution (Ninety-Ninth Amendment) Act, 2014 and the National Judicial Appointments Commission Act, 2014 were brought into effect on 13.04.2015. However, both Acts were challenged in the Supreme Court. The Supreme Court vide Judgment dated 16.10.2015 declared both Acts as unconstitutional and void. The Collegium system as existing prior to the enforcement of the Constitution (Ninety-Ninth Amendment) Act, 2014 was declared to be operative.

This information was given by the Union Minister of Law and Justice, Shri Arjun Ram Meghwal Ji, in a written reply to the Rajya Sabha.

(Release ID: 2288477)
summaryText:
### OVERVIEW  
The Ministry of Law and Justice provided information on judicial appointments and reforms, stating that the 2014 Constitution (Ninety-Ninth Amendment) Act and National Judicial Appointments Commission Act were declared unconstitutional by the Supreme Court in 2015, thereby reinstating the pre-2014 Collegium system for Supreme Court and High Court appointments.  

### KEY HIGHLIGHTS  
- **Constitutional Provisions**: Articles 124, 217, and 224 govern appointments of Supreme Court and High Court judges.  
- **Memorandum of Procedure (MoP)**: Established in 1998 under the Second and Third Judges cases.  
- **Appointment Process**: Initiated by the Chief Justice of India (Supreme Court) or concerned High Court Chief Justice (High Courts), with recommendations from the Supreme Court Collegium (SCC).  
- **2014 Reforms**: The Constitution (Ninety-Ninth Amendment) Act and National Judicial Appointments Commission Act, 2014, were enacted on 13.04.2015 to replace the Collegium system.  
- **Supreme Court Ruling**: On 16.10.2015, the Court declared both Acts unconstitutional and void, restoring the pre-2014 Collegium system.  
- **Consultation Process**: Judicial appointments require collaboration between the Executive and Judiciary, involving consultations at state and central levels.

headline: DIGITISATION OF COURTS IN ODISHA
fullText:
Ministry of Law and Justice

DIGITISATION OF COURTS IN ODISHA

23 JUL 2026 6:44PM by PIB Delhi

The e-Courts Mission Mode Project is being implemented in a phased manner across the country with the objective of strengthening the use of Information and Communication Technology (ICT) in the judicial system. The Department of Justice in close coordination with the eCommittee of Supreme Court of India is implementing the e-Courts Project in a decentralized manner through the respective High Courts.

In the State of Odisha, the e-Courts Project is being implemented under the guidance of the Orissa High Court in all district and subordinate courts. A total of 817 out of 845 courts and 203 out of 215 court complexes have been equipped with ICT hardware for the computerisation of courts under the e-Courts Project Phase III. As on 30.06.2026, a total of 42.96 lakh legacy case records, comprising 24.17 crore pages have been digitised across the High Court and District Courts. Presently, 18 courts in the High Court and 343 courts in district judiciary are functioning in paperless mode by utilizing the PDF-based tools, facilitating digital access to case records and court proceedings.

All 30 District Court websites have been migrated to S3WAAS platform. 320 court establishments across the district judiciary have been successfully migrated from Case Information System (CIS) 3.0 to CIS 4.0. Since 01.01.2026, CIS has consumed data pertaining to 3.10 lakh FIRs and 2.29 lakh chargesheets through the Inter-operable Criminal Justice System (ICJS) platform.

Further, e-Filing 3.0 software has been implemented across all courts and 1.31 lakh cases have been e-filed till 30.06.2026. Video conferencing facilities have also been enabled across all courts in the State and 7.61 lakh virtual hearings have been conducted till 30.06.2026. In addition, 184 eSewa Kendras have been set up across the State to enhance access to justice for citizens in remote areas.

Under the e-Courts Project Phase III (2023 – 2027), the details of fund sanctioned and utilized by the Orissa High Court are as follows:

Financial Year | Sanctioned | Utilized

2023-24 | 9.79 | 6.77

2024-25 | 58.48 | 53.24

2025-26 | 16.09 | 14.12

2026-27 | 7.09 | 0.43*

*Till 17.07.2026.

Under the e-Courts Project, large-scale digitization of pending, disposed of and historical case records is envisaged to ensure long-term preservation, faster retrieval, reduction in physical storage requirements and improved accessibility for litigants, advocates and judicial officers. In addition, the development of a comprehensive digital and paperless court ecosystem is envisaged to enable end-to-end digital court functioning covering the entire lifecycle of a case. Further, a tripartite agreement has been executed among the Orissa High Court, the State Government of Odisha, and the Government of India to obtain necessary fund sanction for expanding digital court services.

This information was given by the Union Minister of Law and Justice, Shri Arjun Ram Meghwal Ji, in a written reply to the Rajya Sabha.

headline: DELAY IN APPOINTMENT OF JUDGES
fullText:
Ministry of Law and Justice

DELAY IN APPOINTMENT OF JUDGES

23 JUL 2026 6:43PM by PIB Delhi

The Judges of the High Courts are appointed under Article 217 and 224 of the Constitution of India and according to the procedure laid down in the Memorandum of Procedure (MoP) prepared in 1998 pursuant to the Supreme Court Judgment of October 6, 1993 (Second Judges case) read with their Advisory Opinion of October 28, 1998 (Third Judges case).

Filling up of vacancies in the higher Judiciary is a continuous, integrated and collaborative process between the executive and the judiciary. As per the Memorandum of Procedure (MoP), the responsibility for initiation of proposals for appointment of Judges in the Supreme Court vests with the Chief Justice of India, while the responsibility for initiation of proposals for appointment of Judges in the High Courts vests with the Chief Justice of the concerned High Court, in consultation with two senior-most puisne Judges of the High Court. As per the MoP, the High Courts are required to make recommendations at least 06 months before the occurrence of a vacancy. However, this time limit is rarely observed. For appointments to the High Courts, the views of concerned State Government are obtained in accordance with the MoP. The recommendations also have to be considered in the light of such other reports as may be available to the Government in respect of the names under consideration. The recommendations of the High Court Collegium, the State Governments and the Government of India are then forwarded to the Supreme Court Collegium (SCC) for advice. Appointment of Judges in the higher judiciary is a continuous, integrated and collaborative process between the executive and the judiciary. It requires consultation and approval from various Constitutional Authorities both at State and Central level. headline: Bihar Chief Minister Shri Samrat Choudhary and Union Minister Shri Jayant Chaudhary Announce Permanent Campus of NSTI (Women), Patna; MSDE and Bihar Government Exchange MoU for Skill India International Centre (SIIC) Patna
fullText:
Ministry of Skill Development and Entrepreneurship

Bihar Chief Minister Shri Samrat Choudhary and Union Minister Shri Jayant Chaudhary Announce Permanent Campus of NSTI (Women), Patna; MSDE and Bihar Government Exchange MoU for Skill India International Centre (SIIC) Patna

23 JUL 2026 6:43PM by PIB Delhi

In a major boost to women’s skill development and global employment opportunities for Bihar’s youth, Chief Minister of Bihar, Shri Samrat Choudhary and Minister of State (Independent Charge) for Skill Development and Entrepreneurship and Minister of State for Education, Government of India, Shri Jayant Chaudhary, today announced the establishment of the permanent campus of the National Skill Training Institute for Women, NSTI (W), Patna and Skill India International, Patna.

The foundation stone for the permanent campus of NSTI (Women), Patna was laid at the project site by Shri Dilip Kumar, Director General, Directorate General of Training (DGT). On the occasion, the Ministry of Skill Development and Entrepreneurship (MSDE), Government of India, and the Government of Bihar also exchanged a Memorandum of Understanding for the establishment of a Skill India International Centre (SIIC) in Bihar.

Together, the two initiatives will strengthen advanced vocational training for women and create structured pathways for Bihar’s youth to access international employment opportunities.

The permanent campus of NSTI (W), Patna, will be developed at Bihta with an investment of ₹99.97 crore. The campus will include a modern Administrative Block, Academic Block and Girls’ Hostel, enabling the institute to substantially expand its training capacity and introduce industry-aligned and future-ready courses.

The proposed Skill India International Centre, supported by a capital investment of approximately ₹7 crore from the Government of India, will prepare Bihar’s youth for global careers through foreign-language training, international employment counselling, certification guidance, pre-departure orientation and safe-migration support.

Shri Samrat Choudhary, Chief Minister said, “The foundation stone laying of the National Skill Training Institute for Women, NSTI (W), at Sikandarpur, Bihta, and the exchange of the MoU between the Government of India and the Government of Bihar for establishing the Skill India International Centre mark an important step in strengthening Bihar’s skilling ecosystem. To be developed at a cost of approximately ₹99.97 crore, the modern NSTI (W) campus will provide young women with quality, industry-aligned training and prepare them for emerging employment opportunities. The Skill India International Centre will similarly equip Bihar’s youth with internationally benchmarked skills and open new pathways to employment in India and overseas. Together, these initiatives will connect our youth with skills, self-reliance and global opportunities, while giving fresh momentum to our shared vision of a Samriddh Bihar and a Viksit Bharat.”

Speaking on the occasion, Shri Jayant Chaudhary said, “Women-led development is central to India’s growth story. The permanent campus of NSTI (W), Patna, reflects our commitment to providing women with access to modern infrastructure, industry-relevant skills and new opportunities. The Skill India International Centre will complement this effort by preparing Bihar’s youth for international careers through globally aligned training, language skills and safe-migration support. Together, these initiatives will strengthen Bihar’s workforce and contribute to the vision of Viksit Bharat.”

NSTI (W), Patna, is the only Central Government skill training institute exclusively for women in Bihar. The institute was inaugurated by Hon’ble Prime, Shri Narendra Modi, has been operating from a temporary campus and offers long-term training under the Craft Instructor Training Scheme (CITS) and Craftsmen Training Scheme (CTS).

The institute from its temporary campus has offered training in Electronics Mechanic, Fashion Design and Technology, Artificial Intelligence and Cosmetology. It has also trained women under initiatives including PM Surya Ghar Muft Bijli Yojana, NIESBUD-led programmes, DGR-EDP, STRIVE and other skill development programmes.

Once operational, the permanent campus will serve as a premier centre for women’s skill development in Bihar and neighbouring States. It will offer modern laboratories, improved residential facilities and industry-aligned training to prepare women for employment, entrepreneurship and emerging opportunities in a rapidly changing economy.

Based on the recommendations of the Trade Selection Committee constituted by DGT, the institute proposes to offer 13 trades under CITS and CTS. These will include conventional and women-centric trades alongside emerging areas such as AI and Programming Assistant, Drone Technician, Solar Technician and Radiology Technician. The proposed courses will provide a total annual training capacity of 435 trainees.

The Skill India International Centre will serve as a dedicated gateway for Bihar’s youth seeking overseas employment. It will offer training in languages such as English, German, Japanese and French, along with international placement counselling, certification assistance and migration support.

The Centre will also provide industry-aligned training in high-demand sectors such as electrical services, plumbing, construction, agriculture and other emerging trades. The training will be aligned with international occupational standards and the workforce requirements of destination countries.

The ceremony was joined by Shri Arun Shankar Prasad, Minister, Labour Resources, Migrant Workers Welfare and Youth, Employment and Skill Development, Government of Bihar, Shri Pratyaya Amrit, Chief Secretary of Bihar, senior officials of the Government of India and the Government of Bihar and representatives of the skill development ecosystem.

(Release ID: 2288475)
summaryText:
### OVERVIEW  
Bihar Chief Minister Shri Samrat Choudhary and Union Minister Shri Jayant Chaudhary announced the permanent campus of the National Skill Training Institute for Women (NSTI (W), Patna) at Bihta, along with the establishment of the Skill India International Centre (SIIC) Patna, following an MoU exchange between the Ministry of Skill Development and Entrepreneurship (MSDE) and the Bihar Government. The NSTI (W) campus, costing ₹99.97 crore, will expand training capacity for women, while the SIIC, funded with ₹7 crore by the Indian government, will prepare youth for global employment through language training and migration support.

In addition, the performance of FTSCs is a regular agenda item in the Inter-State Zonal Council meetings to improve inter-governmental coordination and expedite justice delivery.

As regards the recruitment of judges and staff in courts, filling up of vacant positions of the judicial officers in District and Subordinate courts including the FTSCs, is the responsibility of the State/UT Governments and the concerned High Courts. As per the Constitutional framework, in exercise of powers conferred under proviso to Article 309 read with Articles 233 and 234 of the Constitution, the State/UT Government in consultation with the respective High Court frames the rules regarding the recruitment and appointment of Judicial Officers.

The primary responsibility for the development of infrastructure facilities for District and Subordinate Courts in the States/UTs, rests with the State/UT Governments. However, to augment the resources of the State /UT Governments, the Union Government has been implementing a Centrally Sponsored Scheme for the Development of Infrastructure Facilities for District and Subordinate Courts by providing financial assistance to the State/UT Governments in the prescribed fund sharing between Center and States since 1993-94. At present, there are five components of the Scheme for which Central assistance is granted to the States/UTs viz (i) Court Halls (ii) Residential Units (iii) Lawyers’ Halls (iv) Toilet Complexes and (v) Digital Computer Rooms. An amount of ₹4,519.47 crore has been provided as financial assistance to the States/UTs under the Centrally Sponsored Scheme for the Development of Infrastructure Facilities for Judiciary during the XV Finance Commission cycle.

This information was given by the Union Minister of Law and Justice, Shri Arjun Ram Meghwal Ji, in a written reply to the Rajya Sabha.

headline: EFFECTIVENESS OF FREE LEGAL AID IN JAMMU & KASHMIR
fullText:
Ministry of Law and Justice

EFFECTIVENESS OF FREE LEGAL AID IN JAMMU & KASHMIR

23 JUL 2026 6:40PM by PIB Delhi

The Ministry of Law and Justice has conducted an independent third-party evaluation-cum-impact assessment study of the erstwhile DISHA Scheme (2021-26) through M/s Quality Council of India (QCI), an autonomous body under the Ministry of Commerce and Industry which highlighted the relevance of the Scheme. DISHA Scheme has been extended as DISHA 2.0 for a period of five years (2026-31), for pan India implementation, including Jammu & Kashmir.

The DISHA 2.0 Scheme provides easy, accessible, affordable, technology enabled and people-centric legal services through its components viz. Tele-Law (Reaching the Unreached), Nyaya Bandhu (Pro Bono Legal Services) Programme, Legal Literacy and Legal Awareness Programme, and VIDHI (Vision for Integrated Delivery of Harmonised Legal Initiatives) – Sanjeevani.

The Tele-Law connects people with panel lawyers through mobile-app and Toll Free No. 14454 for rendering pre-litigation legal advice and through use of video/tele conferencing facilities available at the Common Service Centres (CSCs)/Village Level Entrepreneurs (VLEs) at the panchayat level. Nyaya Sahayaks, who are active community based legal facilitators, have been engaged in the Aspirational Blocks to strengthen service delivery.

As on 30.06.2026, 7,63,148 pre-litigation legal advice have been provided by 5 panel lawyers, facilitated through CSCs in the UT of Jammu & Kashmir. 8 Nyaya Sahayaks working in 10 Aspirational Blocks have registered 1,222 calls.

The Nyaya Bandhu (Pro-Bono Legal Services) Programme promotes a pro bono culture among advocates and law students by facilitating free legal assistance to eligible beneficiaries under Section 12 of the Legal Services Authorities (LSA) Act, 1987, through the Nyaya Bandhu Portal and Mobile Application, which enables seamless connect between registered beneficiaries and Pro Bono Advocates. As on 30.06.2026, 197 advocates are registered under the Bar Council of Jammu & Kashmir.

The Legal Literacy and Legal Awareness Programme (LLLAP) envisages engaging with institutions and building partnerships across stakeholder Ministries/Departments, to build capacity of the grassroot workers, legally empower them and spread legal awareness through workshops, webinars, IEC materials, collaboration with Prasar Bharati and others.

VIDHI (Vision for Integrated Delivery of Harmonized Legal Initiatives) - Sanjeevani is envisaged as a key component under DISHA 2.0 to enhance the monitoring mechanism of the scheme through development and operationalization of a centralized Integrated Dashboard. The dashboard aims to provide a unified interface integrating real-time data streams from multiple sources to facilitate monitoring of the programme performance, service delivery and implementation progress. It envisages integration with the Nyaya Setu AI Chatbot to facilitate technology-enabled service support and improve user interaction through AI-driven assistance mechanisms. VIDHI Sanjeevani envisages outreach to 50 lakh beneficiaries over a period of five years i.e., 2026-31.

This information was given by the Union Minister of Law and Justice, Shri Arjun Ram Meghwal Ji, in a written reply to the Rajya Sabha.

(Release ID: 2288469)

The Ministry of Law and Justice has extended the DISHA Scheme as DISHA 2.0 for five years (2026-31) with pan-India implementation, including Jammu & Kashmir, following a third-party evaluation by M/s Quality Council of India. The scheme aims to deliver accessible, technology-enabled legal services through four components: Tele-Law, Nyaya Bandhu, Legal Literacy and Awareness Programme, and VIDHI-Sanjeevani.

headline: IPC Strengthens Viksit UP–Viksit Bharat Vision Through Strategic MoUs to Advance Healthcare Quality and Scientific Innovation
fullText:
Ministry of Health and Family Welfare

IPC Strengthens Viksit UP–Viksit Bharat Vision Through Strategic MoUs to Advance Healthcare Quality and Scientific Innovation

IPC and CSIR-CIMAP Renew Collaboration to Advance Medicinal Plant Research, Standardisation and Quality Evaluation of Herbal Medicines

IPC and DSMNRU Collaborate to Strengthen Assistive Device Quality Assessment and Promote Sign Language-Based Pharmacovigilance

23 JUL 2026 6:39PM by PIB Delhi

The Indian Pharmacopoeia Commission (IPC), an autonomous institution under the Ministry of Health & Family Welfare, Government of India, reinforced its commitment to the vision of Viksit UP–Viksit Bharat by signing and renewing two Memoranda of Understanding (MoUs) with Dr. Shakuntala Misra National Rehabilitation University (DSMNRU), Lucknow, and CSIR-Central Institute of Medicinal and Aromatic Plants (CSIR-CIMAP).

The ceremony was attended by Prof. Sanjay Singh, Vice-Chancellor, DSMNRU, Dr. V. Kalaiselvan, Secretary-cum-Scientific Director, IPC, and senior officials from CSIR-CIMAP.

The renewed MoU with CSIR-CIMAP will continue collaboration in medicinal plant research, standardization, and quality evaluation of herbal medicines.

The new MoU with DSMNRU will focus on developing scientific protocols for assessing the quality, safety, and performance of assistive devices used by persons with disabilities. The collaboration will also support the development of a pharmacovigilance training module in sign language to enhance medicine safety awareness and adverse drug reaction reporting among persons with disabilities.

Speaking on the occasion, Dr. V. Kalaiselvan said that these collaborations will strengthen scientific research, quality standards, and institutional partnerships, supporting Uttar Pradesh’s emergence as a centre of excellence in healthcare and pharmaceutical quality. He emphasized that such initiatives contribute directly to the shared vision of Viksit UP–Viksit Bharat by improving access to safe, effective, and quality healthcare products.

These partnerships reaffirm IPC’s commitment to advancing quality healthcare, scientific innovation, and capacity building in support of national healthcare priorities.

headline: LEGAL REFORMS
fullText:
Ministry of Law and Justice

23 JUL 2026 6:39PM by PIB Delhi

The Central Government is committed for speedy disposal of cases and reducing pendency as mandated under Article 21 of the Constitution and has taken several initiatives to provide an ecosystem for faster disposal of cases by the judiciary:

The Phase-III of the eCourts Project (2023-2027) was approved on 13.09.2023 with an outlay of Rs. 7,210 crore to make justice more accessible. More than 4,14,89,062 court hearings have been conducted through video conferencing and live streaming is functional in several High Courts. The number of e-Sewa Kendras (facilitation centres) providing free eCourts services has increased to 2,515 across High Courts and District Courts.

Under the Centrally Sponsored Scheme namely Fast Track Special Courts (FTSCs) Scheme, for the expeditious disposal of pending cases of Rape and POCSO Act, 775 Fast Track Special Courts (FTSCs) including 398 exclusive POCSO (ePOCSO) Courts are functional across 29 States/UTs as on 30.04.2026. Till 30.04.2026, Fast Track Special Courts have collectively disposed of 3,87,481 cases.

Arrears Committees have been set up in all 25 High Courts and the District Courts to clear cases pending for more than five years.

The Government has also enacted The Negotiable Instruments (Amendment) Act, 2018, The Commercial Courts (Amendment) Act, 2018, The Specific Relief (Amendment) Act, 2018 with a view to reduce pendency. In addition, New Criminal Laws 2023 have been enacted.

Alternate Dispute Resolution methods have also been promoted by the Government. The Commercial Courts Act, 2015 was amended in August, 2018 making Pre-institution Mediation and Settlement (PIMS) mandatory in case of commercial disputes. Amendment to the Arbitration and Conciliation Act, 1996 has been made by the Arbitration and Conciliation (Amendment) Act 2015 for expediting the speedy resolution of disputes by prescribing timelines.

Lok Adalat is an important Alternative Disputes Resolution Mechanism available to common people, where the disputes/ cases pending in the court of law or at pre-litigation stage are settled/compromised amicably.

The Department of Justice has been implementing a Central Sector Scheme, namely, Designing Innovative Solutions for Holistic Access to Justice (DISHA) 2.0, with a total outlay of Rs. 255 crores for a period of five years (2026-2031) for providing a comprehensive, integrated solution on Access to Justice at a pan India level through Tele-Law (pre-litigation legal advice through telephone), Nyaya Bandhu (Pro-bono Advocates), Legal Literacy & Legal Awareness and VIDHI (Vision for Integrated Delivery of Harmonised Legal Initiatives)- Sanjeevani Programmes. As on 30th June, 2026, more than 1.13 crore pre-litigation legal advice have been provided across the country. As on 30th June, 2026, total 10,835 advocates have volunteered and registered on Nyaya Bandhu platform. Nyaya Setu AI Chatbot facilitates automated query handling, structured data capture, and multilingual interaction support through BHASHINI-enabled functionalities, ensuring language is not a barrier to justice-related services.

headline: Department of Telecommunications Activates Intra Circle Roaming in Jammu & Kashmir, Assam and Nagaland amid Heavy Rainfall, Cloudburst and Landslides
fullText:
Ministry of Communications

Department of Telecommunications Activates Intra Circle Roaming in Jammu & Kashmir, Assam and Nagaland amid Heavy Rainfall, Cloudburst and Landslides

Measure Aimed at Ensuring Uninterrupted Telecom Connectivity during Disaster Situation

23 JUL 2026 6:30PM by PIB Delhi

Department of Telecommunications (DoT) has taken swift measures to ensure continuity of telecom services in Jammu & Kashmir, Assam and Nagaland following severe disruption of communication networks caused by heavy rainfall, cloudburst and landslides.

Recognizing the critical role of telecommunications during disasters, DoT immediately activated its emergency response mechanism and issued directions to all Telecom Service Providers for enabling Intra-Circle Roaming (ICR) in the following districts of these 3 States/UT:-

Jammu & Kashmir – Poonch and Rajouri

Assam - Sibsagar, Charaideo and Jorhat

The activation of ICR will allow mobile subscribers of any telecom service provider to automatically access another available network in locations where their home network is temporarily unavailable. This facility will also be available to subscribers visiting these districts from other parts of the country. The measure is expected to significantly enhance communication availability for affected residents, disaster response teams, healthcare personnel, and government agencies engaged in relief and rescue operations.

The Department of Telecommunications is closely monitoring the situation through its field units and is coordinating with Telecom Service Providers for expeditious restoration of telecom services in the affected areas.

What is ICR & how to use it:

Intra Circle Roaming (ICR) is a telecom arrangement where a mobile subscriber connects to another operator’s network within the same telecom circle (region). This allows users to access mobile services even if their own operator has no coverage in that specific area. It plays a critical role during natural disasters when mobile infrastructure is damaged.

During the time of disaster, if mobile infrastructure is damaged in a region, DoT issues instructions to Telecom Service Operators to implement ICR.

To ensure ICR works:

Keep "Automatic Network Selection" enabled in your phone settings.

If automatic connection fails;

Go to Settings > Mobile Networks > Network Operators

Manually select another available network

(Release ID: 2288459)

summaryText:
### OVERVIEW
The Department of Telecommunications has activated Intra Circle Roaming (ICR) in designated districts of Jammu & Kashmir, Assam and Nagaland to restore telecom services amid severe weather‑induced disruptions.

### KEY HIGHLIGHTS
- ICR was activated on 23 July 2026 to address network outages caused by heavy rainfall, cloudburst and landslides.  
- Coverage includes Poonch and Rajouri (J&K) and Sibsagar, Charaideo and Jorhat (Assam), plus designated districts in Nagaland.  
- Enables any mobile subscriber to automatically connect to an available network when the home network is unavailable.  
- Supports connectivity for residents, disaster‑response teams, healthcare personnel and government agencies.  
- DoT will monitor the situation through field units and coordinate with telecom service providers for rapid restoration.

headline: Ministry of Home Affairs (MHA) proactively monitoring monsoon situation across the country
fullText:
Ministry of Home Affairs

Ministry of Home Affairs (MHA) proactively monitoring monsoon situation across the country

Under the leadership of Prime Minister Shri Narendra Modi and guidance of Union Home Minister Shri Amit Shah, MHA is providing all necessary support to affected States and Union Territories in managing challenges posed by heavy rains

Besides disseminating Early Warnings up to States/UTs and Districts, Control Room monitoring all rescue activities round the clock

Another Inter-Ministerial Central Team is scheduled to visit affected area of Assam from 25th July 2026 for assessing loss and damage caused due to incessant rains and floods

23 JUL 2026 6:08PM by PIB Delhi

The Ministry of Home Affairs (MHA) is proactively monitoring the monsoon situation across the country. Under the leadership of Prime Minister Shri Narendra Modi and guidance of Union Home Minister Shri Amit Shah, MHA is providing all necessary support to affected States and Union Territories in managing challenges posed by heavy rains.

In the month of July, 2026, country has faced heavy rains and landslides in some parts like Nagaland, Arunachal Pradesh, West Bengal, Assam, Gujarat, Maharashtra, Dadar and Nagar Haveli, Daman, J& K etc.

The Ministry of Home Affairs Integrated Control Room (ICR-ER) is working in close coordination with other central agencies including India Meteorological Department (IMD), Central Water Commission (CWC) and Indian National Centre for Ocean Information Services (INCOIS) for raising timely alerts to State Emergency Operation Centres and District Emergency Operation Centres. Besides disseminating Early Warnings up to States/UTs and Districts, the Control Room monitoring all the rescue activities round the clock.

National Disaster Response Force (NDRF) has worked tirelessly with State Government Agencies, State police and SDRF in all these places for rescue and relief operations to save human lives. NDRF pre-positioned its teams at vulnerable places to reduce response time. During the last one week, 52 civilians have been rescued by NDRF teams whereas 2206 civilians were evacuated to safer places across all the affected States/UTs.

In coordination with MHA, the Ministry of Defence (MoD) also mobilized its resources within shortest possible time to save stranded people, to provide assistance to Rescue Agencies in transportation of men and material and distributing relief materials.

A review meeting regarding preparedness for pre-monsoon floods and heatwaves across the country, was chaired by Union Home Minister Shri Amit Shah on 10th May, 2026. In follow up, another meeting was held by MHA on 03rd June, 2026 to review the overall preparedness on heavy rain, cloudburst, floods and landslides in Jammu and Kashmir. Preparedness along the Char Dham yatra route was also reviewed by MHA regularly.

Under directions of Union Home Minister Shri Amit Shah, the Inter-Ministerial Central Teams have already visited the affected areas of Arunachal Pradesh and Assam from 07th to 10th July 2026, for assessing the extent of damage and to provide necessary financial assistance to these States from NDRF. Another Inter-Ministerial Central Team is scheduled to visit the affected area of Assam from 25.07.2026 for assessing loss and damage caused due to incessant rains and floods.

Though recent rains have caused difficulty to people in some states, overall it has reduced the earlier predicted rain deficit of approximately 30 percentage to about 15 percentage.

### OVERVIEW  
The Ministry of Home Affairs (MHA) is proactively managing the monsoon crisis across India under the leadership of Prime Minister Shri Narendra Modi and Union Home Minister Shri Amit Shah, coordinating with central agencies to issue alerts, conduct rescue operations, and provide financial assistance to affected states and union territories.  

### KEY HIGHLIGHTS  
- MHA is coordinating with central agencies like IMD, CWC, and INCOIS to issue timely alerts to state and district emergency bodies.  
- The Integrated Control Room (ICR-ER) monitors rescue activities 24/7.  
- National Disaster Response Force (NDRF) has rescued 52 civilians and evacuated 2,206 individuals in the past week.  
- Inter-Ministerial Central Teams visited Arunachal Pradesh and Assam from 7–10 July 2026 to assess damage and provide financial assistance.  
- A second Inter-Ministerial Central Team is scheduled to visit Assam on 25 July 2026 for further assessment.  
- A review of monsoon preparedness was chaired by Union Home Minister Shri Shah on 10 May 2026.  
- Pre-monsoon preparedness reviews were conducted for Jammu & Kashmir on 3 June 2026.  
- Heavy rains and landslides affected regions including Nagaland, Arunachal Pradesh, Assam, West Bengal, Gujarat, Maharashtra, and Jammu & Kashmir.  
- Early monsoon rains reduced the predicted rain deficit from 30% to 15%.
